title: Hippeastrum restingense
taxonomy: Hippeastrum restingense is classified as a species of flowering bulb plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of the order Asparagales and the clade monocots. Within the Amaryllidaceae family, Hippeastrum restingense is a member of the subfamily Amaryllidoideae. This plant's classification also places it within the genus Hippeastrum, a group of plants commonly known as the Amaryllis. Further classification puts Hippeastrum restingense within the section Hippeastrum, a subset of the genus.

intro: Hippeastrum retusum, also known as the Red Spider Lily, is a species of flowering bulbous plant native to South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Hippeastrum retusum include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, such as galanthamine and hippeastrine, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
